name: French Southern and Antarctic Lands (France)

colors.symbolism: the Southern Ocean and the deep waters around the territories; the French naval tradition
the white of the French tricolour and the snowfields of the Antarctic and sub-Antarctic islands; the TAAF letters and the stars
the red of the French tricolour

description: A blue field with a white canton in the upper hoist containing a miniature French tricolour, and in the fly the white letters 'TAAF' surrounded by five white five-pointed stars representing the five districts of the territory.
history: Adopted in 2007 as the official flag of the Terres australes et antarctiques françaises (French Southern and Antarctic Lands) — the overseas territory of France comprising several sub-Antarctic island groups, Adélie Land in Antarctica, and the Scattered Islands in the Indian Ocean. France's claims in Antarctica are governed by the Antarctic Treaty System.

features.symbolism: the blue field of the surrounding Southern Ocean and historic association with the French Navy
white canton bearing a small French tricolour, indicating French sovereignty over the territory
miniature French tricolour within the white canton — sovereignty of France
The letters 'TAAF' (Terres australes et antarctiques françaises) in white, identifying the territory.

Five white five-pointed stars representing the five districts of the territory: the Crozet Islands, the Kerguelen Islands, Saint Paul and Amsterdam Islands, Adélie Land in Antarctica, and the Scattered Islands in the Indian Ocean.
